Fig. 7. Resource consumption comparison between EvoTSC and baseline methods on the ECG5000 dataset.
Fig. 3. Distribution of accuracy improvement over the raw data baseline across all datasets.
Fig. 2. Illustration of the proposed multi-layer program structure (a) and an evolved feature learning model example (b).

Fig. 5. Heatmap of operation proportions across different dataset groups. Datasets are grouped by number of classes, time-series length, and data type. Segment Dectection (Seg.): SegDect indicates the proportion of branches that use segment detection. Domain Transform: Raw denotes the proportion of branches that operate directly in the original domain without applying any transformation; Freq and Diff denote frequency transform and differencing, respectively. Content-Adaptive Patching: D = i indicates that the length of patches is ℓin i ; No Patch indicates that no patching operation is applied in the branch. Feature Extraction: values represent the proportions of different extraction operator, where Shape aggregates all shape-related extraction operations.

Fig. 6. Case study on the ECG5000 dataset. (a) Visualization of the training instances, where different colors denote different classess. (b) Two evolved individuals, where each SegDect node has a left terminal indicating the segment length and a right terminal indicating the starting position.
